rag_rules: Place input assumptions in component type annexes when bounds are concrete or visible
Use assign only for implementation-level bindings with existing output features or auxiliary variables
Avoid placing guarantees in implementation scope; use type scope for input assumptions and output guarantees
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope; otherwise omit the clause
generic_rules: Use visible identifiers from the current scope only
Align helper declaration types with expression types
Avoid encoding AADL properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed
Use concrete bounds or visible relations for range assumptions and guarantees
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `eq` for equation declarations and `assign` for implementation-level bindings
Comparison expressions are bool; integer literals and arithmetic are int; real literals and arithmetic are real
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantees in implementation scope
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit exposure
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons
